channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Sudanese paramilitary group says its forming a rival government
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A notorious paramilitary group fighting against the Sudanese military announced that it was forming a rival government, which will rule parts of the country controlled by the group including the western Darfur region where the United Nations says recent attacks by the group have killed
item.dc:creator: BY SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, say he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: UK Supreme Court rules that equalities law defines a woman as someone born biologically female
item.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that the U.K. equalities law defines a woman as someone born biologically female.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An international conference rallies aid for Sudan after 2 years of civil war, but peace is elusive
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain and the European Union pledged hundreds of millions of dollars Tuesday to ease suffering in Sudan, on the second anniversary of a civil war that has killed tens of thousands of people, displaced 14 million and pushed large parts of the country into famine.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France expels 12 Algerian officials in tit-for-tat move amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France said Tuesday it was expelling 12 Algerian diplomatic officials a day after Algeria announced the expulsion of the same number of French officials in escalating tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US blasts UN for extending human rights envoy to Palestinian territories
item.description: The United States is rebuking a United Nations decision to extend Francesca Albanese’s tenure as the U.N. special rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike outside a Gaza hospital kills a medic and wounds several
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of the Kuwaiti Field Hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people — all patients and medics — according to a spokesman for the hospital in the Muwasi area, where hundreds of thousands
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: A Russian court has convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top Hamas terror leader killed in 'precise strike' by Israel: IDF
item.description: Top Hamas leader Muhammad al-‘Ajlah has been killed in an Israeli strike, the IDF has announced. Al-'Ajilah was responsible for arming terrorists with weapons used to carry out attacks against Israeli civilians and IDF troops, according to the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary passes constitutional amendment to ban LGBTQ+ public events, seen as a major blow to rights
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary’s parliament on Monday passed an amendment to the constitution that allows the government to ban public events by LGBTQ+ communities, a decision that legal scholars and critics call another step toward authoritarianism by the populist government.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singapore dissolves parliament, paving the way for May 3 general election
item.description: Singapore’s parliament was dissolved Tuesday, paving the way for general elections in which the city-state’s long-ruling People’s Action Party will seek to strengthen its dominance under Prime Minister Lawrence Wong.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York City's mayor visits the Dominican Republic after the deadly nightclub roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams arrived in the Dominican Republic on Monday to pay his respects to the scores of people who died last week when the roof of a popular nightclub collapsed in the country's capital, Santo Domingo.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attack by gunmen leaves at least 40 people dead in Nigeria, the country's president says
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — Nigeria's president said on Monday that at least 40 people were killed when Muslim gunmen, believed to be herders, attacked a Christian farming community in the north-central part of the country, the latest in an increasing wave of violence in the West African country.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Malaysian leader Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, who extended political freedoms, dies at 85
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Former Malaysian Prime Minister Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, a moderate who extended the country’s political freedoms but was criticized for lackluster leadership, has died of heart disease. He was 85.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump trade war with China hurting US farmers, advocate says
item.description: John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation family farmer who works 1,300 acres in Virginia, said he hopes the Trump administration will listen to farmers moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin secures release of American missionary held in Tunisia for 13 months: report
item.description: American missionary Robert Vieira, detained in Tunisia for more than a year, is set to return home soon after officials secured his release on Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fears of another narco-state rise as Latin American country readies for pivotal vote
item.description: Ecuador is headed to a second round of a presidential election in which incumbent President Daniel Noboa faces leftist challenger Luisa González as the nation faces gang violence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a penguin in a cardboard box caused a helicopter crash in South Africa
item.description: A penguin inside a carboard box led to a helicopter crash in South Africa, the country's Civil Aviation Authority said in a report this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9
